title: DUPRACETAM
description: Dupracetam is a synthetic nootropic compound belonging to the racetam family, structurally characterized as a hydrazide derivative. It is hypothesized to modulate neurotransmission to enhance cognitive function, though one of its metabolites, 1-methylhydantoin, has been noted for potential renal toxicity at high dosages.

research.title: Efficacy and safety of oxiracetam in patients with vascular cognitive impairment: A multicenter, randomized, double-blinded, placebo-controlled, phase IV clinical trial
research.summary: This high-level trial demonstrated that racetam analogues can significantly improve cognitive symptoms and daily living activities in patients with vascular impairment

research.title: Cognitive effects of piracetam in adults with memory impairment: A systematic review and meta-analysis
research.summary: A comprehensive review confirming that the parent racetam structure provides measurable benefits for memory and general cognitive function in impaired adults

sentiment.summary: Users are often cautious due to the lack of human data and the known renal toxicity of its metabolite, leading to split opinions on its safety versus efficacy.

effects.label: Potency:
effects.description: Generally considered more potent than piracetam but less documented than oxiracetam

effects.label: Reliability:
effects.description: Anecdotal evidence suggests varied responses with some users feeling no discernible effect

effects.label: Comparison:
effects.description: Often compared to other hydrazide racetams regarding its unique metabolic pathway

dosage.label: Standard Range:
dosage.description: Typical anecdotal doses range from 20-50mg due to its high potency relative to older racetams

dosage.label: Frequency:
dosage.description: Often taken once daily to avoid accumulation of metabolites

dosage.label: Caution:
dosage.description: Users emphasize starting with conservative doses to monitor for renal discomfort

sideEffects.label: Renal Toxicity:
sideEffects.description: Risk of kidney stress due to the metabolite 1-methylhydantoin at high doses

sideEffects.label: Headaches:
sideEffects.description: Common 'racetam headache' often managed with choline supplementation

sideEffects.label: Insomnia:
sideEffects.description: Potential for sleep disruption if taken late in the day due to stimulatory effects

availability.label: Research Chemical status:
availability.description: Often sold as a 'not for human consumption' compound in the nootropic market

availability.label: Sourcing:
availability.description: Primarily available through specialized online synthetic chemical suppliers

availability.label: Legality:
availability.description: Generally unscheduled in most jurisdictions but not approved by the FDA for medical use
